channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: US House votes down latest effort to curtail Trump’s power to wage Iran war
item.description: Nearly all Democrats voted in favour, a day after a similar resolution failed in the US Senate along party lines.

item.title: Ex-Arsenal and Liverpool keeper Manninger killed in car crash with train
item.description: Alex Manninger played for Arsenal, Liverpool and Juventus at peak of his career while winning 33 Austria caps.

item.title: ‘Whose is he?’: A Gaza father’s fight for a child who might not be his
item.description: Mohammed Lubbad's wife Amal was pregnant when she died in Israeli air strikes. Then he was told she had given birth.

item.title: No date set for US-Iran talks, as Pakistan pushes to keep diplomacy alive
item.description: Pakistan's prime minister and army chief are both travelling to key players in the war, attempting to keep talks going.

item.title: US general clarifies Iranian ports under blockade, not Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A US general had to clarify that the US military is only blockading Iranian ports, not the entire Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Jet fuel shortage: Why Iran war could ground flights in Europe
item.description: Europe's aviation faces fuel shortages as Hormuz tensions threaten travel at the start of the summer season.

item.title: US strike on Pacific vessel kills three
item.description: US military footage released Wednesday shows its latest strike on a vessel in the eastern Pacific, killing three people.

item.title: Turkiye’s Roketsan eyes top 10 exporter rank amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Turkiye has rapidly expanded its defence industry to become a leading global missile exporter amid rising conflict.

item.title: Pentagon chief Hegseth warns Iran blockade to last ‘as long as takes’
item.description: US urges resolution through talks, but says Washington 'locked and loaded' to resume attacks.

item.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
item.description: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.
